spec_match.rationale: Floater now applies anchor-size() in `margin-left`, `min-width`, and `max-width`, matching the visible code sample and the spec's margin/inset expansion.

interactive.rationale: Slider drives card scale; live readout shows measured card width, margin-left, actual/natural floater width, and computed min/max limits.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises a proportional design-system spacing pattern, the page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: The page now exposes a visible support/fallback note and readouts that show min/max clamping at small and large card scales.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout; rose-on-ivory and emerald-on-ivory both pass WCAG AA; no static-card pattern.

summary: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome/148.0.0.0 at http://localhost:3000/v131/css-anchor-positioning-allow-anchor-size-in-inset-and-margin-properties/safe-area-pad/. Before the fix, the code sample included max-width but the live computed maxWidth was `none`. After the fix, the stylesheet includes `max-width: calc(anchor-size(--card width) * 0.8)`, Chrome supports that declaration, and slider samples settled at 0.7x / 1.0x / 1.6x with computed max-width values of 96.0px / 135.8px / 215.4px. The readout also shows actual versus natural floater width and min/max limits. Console had no warnings/errors and network requests succeeded.
